name: Scorer Validation Relevance review for Acceptable Use Operational Training Augmentation Update bundle
summary: Accepted. The claim is relevant to automated-scoring development and validation workflows while preserving the distinction between training augmentation and final validation. It explicitly denies synthetic-only deployment-readiness, subgroup fairness, cut-score, and operational decision support.

findings.claim_or_issue: Operational training augmentation can improve model development evidence but is not itself final validation evidence.
findings.why_it_matters: This is the central distinction needed for automated scoring and public assessment claims.
findings.recommended_action: Retain the staged interpretive note and does_not_apply_when language.
